group.name: User-flagged defect recorded only in untracked audit output

statement: CLAUDE.md line 100 autonomous-loop paragraph user-flagged and assistant-confirmed as not clear and not easy to follow; no clarity change landed by pinned sha and the only records of the defect are gitignored session-audit atoms — no tracked issue, gap, or committed record exists
action: Create a durable tracked record of the line-100 legibility defect (committed record or substrate filing, per the user's disposition), then restructure the paragraph for legibility with zero change to what it authorizes or forbids — the audit atom's dispatch scope already pins this: substance of the mandate-008 exception out of bounds, iteration-cap language (removed at commit 82500702) must not be reintroduced. The user gave no disposition before session end; the how/whether is the user's call — the drop is the absence of any tracked record.

in: a durable tracked record of the defect
out: any change to the substance of the mandate-008 exception, deterministic-gate definition, or audit-posture rules

verify: awk 'NR==100' /Users/david/Projects/workflowsPiExtension/CLAUDE.md — confirm the single-line run-on paragraph is unchanged; git -C /Users/david/Projects/workflowsPiExtension grep -in 'clarity' -- CLAUDE.md analysis .context — confirm no tracked record of the line-100 legibility defect; git -C /Users/david/Projects/workflowsPiExtension check-ignore projects .session-audits — confirm both audit-output locations are ignored, i.e. the only existing records are untracked.

history.note: new-window discovery: user flagged 2026-07-18T17:17Z after the 82500702 cap-clause deletion; session ended 2026-07-19T04:48Z with no disposition; the 2026-07-19 five-session audit lists it as still-open item 7 with no tracked to-do anywhere

action: Reconstruct the four gap payloads from the committed analysis (analysis/2026-07-13-wave3-residual-filings.md, which the audit report states verdicts all four file-new and preserves their substance) and file them via append-block-item with the birth relations the report names (FGAP-066/148 lineage; FGAP-144 context; FGAP-120/151/147/149/089 family; FGAP-129 class), then context-validate. The in-window 2026-07-15 revert of an audit-agent .context filing was a procedural rejection of that filing, not a retirement of the content — the committed report records no later user statement retiring the filings.

out: FGAP-066/120/148/151 themselves (existing filed family items, different content)
re-litigating the user's 2026-07-15 rejection of the audit-agent filing mechanics
